body:
BLOCKED ON A RHIZOMATIC CONVERSATION (rhizomatic is frozen — SPEC hard limit; a substrate change is a PR in that repo + Myk's call). Filed so the idea is not lost. Myk raised it 2026-07-21 after T63 landed the LensName/ProgramName brands in Loam.

THE IDEA. T63 brands `LensName`/`ProgramName` in Loam and adds a `no-restricted-syntax` ESLint rule as a backstop. The lint rule exists for exactly ONE reason: rhizomatic types `HyperSchema.name` as bare `string`, so a raw `r.hyperschema.name === <aLensName>` slips the type checker (string vs a brand is allowed) — the only hole Loam cannot close from its own side. If rhizomatic branded its own name type, that comparison would be `ProgramName === LensName` AT THE SOURCE, a compile error, and the entire lint rule could be DELETED.

THE CLEAN SPLIT. `ProgramName` (≈ `HyperSchema.name`) is the SUBSTRATE's own concept and belongs in rhizomatic. `LensName` is LOAM's — a lens is §21.7 serving-side vocabulary (a reading over a hyperschema) that rhizomatic does not have, so it stays here. rhizomatic branding just its own name type as distinct from arbitrary strings is enough: Loam's `LensName` is then non-overlapping with it for free, and the comparison error falls out.

WHY IT IS WORTH A SUBSTRATE CHANGE RATHER THAN LIVING WITH THE LINT. The whole T63 argument is that the SYSTEM should catch the mistake, not the model. A lint rule is a weaker guarantee than a type — it is scoped to named files, catches only the direct comparison form (see the T63 prosecutor finding), and is a second mechanism that can drift from the first. A branded substrate type is airtight and needs no Loam-side maintenance. This is the "make the wrong thing unrepresentable" tier of defense applied at the layer that owns the type.

WHAT TO ASK RHIZOMATIC (the conversation, not a decision to make here):
  - Is `HyperSchema.name` (and the `schema:<name>` living-entity name, and any other name field) worth branding in the substrate, or is a bare string deliberate there?
  - Does rhizomatic already have, or want, a nominal-string convention? If it brands its names, does Loam's `LensName` compose cleanly (a lens name is derived from a schema's own `name`, so the two brands meet at `lensOf`)?
  - Scope: the smallest change is branding ONLY the name fields, not every string in rhizomatic.

IF IT LANDS UPSTREAM, the Loam follow-up is small and mechanical: retype `programOf` to return rhizomatic's brand (or drop it if `hyperschema.name` is already branded), delete the ESLint rule and its rail declaration on T63, and confirm the full suite stays green (brands still erase at runtime). Note in the journal that the lint backstop was a bridge until the substrate closed the hole.

CARE: DO NOT edit rhizomatic from this repo. This ticket is a placeholder for a conversation + an upstream PR. It has no Loam deliverable until that lands.
title: Push the name brands into rhizomatic and delete the H6 lint backstop (blocked on a rhizomatic conversation)
